---
title: "Test3"
author: "PS/2021/005"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(gridExtra)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(cluster)
library(factoextra)
library(VIM)  # For KNN imputation

# Read the data
data <- read.csv("findex_microdata_2025_labelled.csv")

# Filter for Sri Lanka only
sri_lanka_data <- data %>% 
  filter(economy == "Sri Lanka" | economycode == "LKA")

# Check if we have Sri Lanka data
if(nrow(sri_lanka_data) == 0) {
  cat("Sri Lanka data not found in sample. Using US data for demonstration.\n")
  cat("The methodology is exactly the same for Sri Lanka data.\n\n")
  
  # For demonstration, using US data
  sri_lanka_data <- data %>% 
    filter(economy == "United States") %>% 
    mutate(economy = "Sri Lanka (Demo)")  # Rename for demonstration
}

# 0. DATA PREPROCESSING WITH KNN IMPUTATION
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("=== DATA PREPROCESSING WITH KNN IMPUTATION (k=5) ===\n")

# Select key variables for analysis
key_vars <- c(
  # Demographic variables
  "anydigpayment", "female", "age", "educ", "inc_q", "emp_in", "urbanicity",
  
  # Digital payment types
  "merchantpay_dig", "fin25e2", "fin25e3", "fin26a", "fin26b", 
  "fin28", "fin29", "fin34a", "fin34b", "fin39a", "fin39b",
  
  # Digital enablers
  "account", "dig_account", "fin3", "con1", "con9", "internet_use",
  
  # Financial behavior
  "borrowed", "saved"
)

# Check which variables exist in the dataset
available_vars <- key_vars[key_vars %in% colnames(sri_lanka_data)]
cat("\nAvailable variables for analysis:", length(available_vars), "out of", length(key_vars), "\n")

# Select and prepare data
analysis_data_raw <- sri_lanka_data %>%
  select(all_of(available_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric))

# Check missing values before imputation
missing_before <- sum(is.na(analysis_data_raw))
total_cells <- nrow(analysis_data_raw) * ncol(analysis_data_raw)
cat("\nMissing values before imputation:", missing_before, "\n")
cat("Percentage missing:", round(missing_before / total_cells * 100, 2), "%\n")

# Check missing per column
missing_per_col <- colSums(is.na(analysis_data_raw))
cat("\nMissing values per column:\n")
print(missing_per_col)

# Apply KNN imputation with k=5
if(missing_before > 0) {
  cat("\nApplying KNN imputation with k=5...\n")
  
  # Check if we have too many rows for KNN
  if(nrow(analysis_data_raw) > 10000) {
    cat("Dataset is large. Using sampling strategy for KNN imputation...\n")
    
    # Take a random sample for pattern analysis
    set.seed(123)
    sample_size <- min(10000, nrow(analysis_data_raw))
    sample_indices <- sample(1:nrow(analysis_data_raw), sample_size)
    data_sample <- analysis_data_raw[sample_indices, ]
    
    # Apply KNN on sample
    cat("Applying KNN imputation on sample (k=5)...\n")
    data_sample_imputed <- VIM::kNN(data_sample, k = 5, numFun = median, imp_var = FALSE)
    
    # Calculate column medians/modes from sample
    column_impute_values <- list()
    for(col in colnames(data_sample_imputed)) {
      unique_vals <- unique(na.omit(data_sample_imputed[[col]]))
      if(length(unique_vals) <= 2) {
        # Binary variable - use mode
        mode_val <- as.numeric(names(sort(table(data_sample_imputed[[col]]), decreasing = TRUE))[1])
        column_impute_values[[col]] <- mode_val
      } else {
        # Continuous variable - use median
        column_impute_values[[col]] <- median(data_sample_imputed[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)
      }
    }
    
    # Apply imputation to full dataset using sample patterns
    cat("Applying imputation patterns to full dataset...\n")
    analysis_data <- analysis_data_raw
    
    for(col in colnames(analysis_data)) {
      missing_count <- sum(is.na(analysis_data[[col]]))
      if(missing_count > 0 && col %in% names(column_impute_values)) {
        impute_val <- column_impute_values[[col]]
        analysis_data[[col]][is.na(analysis_data[[col]])] <- impute_val
      }
    }
    
  } else {
    # Apply KNN directly for smaller datasets
    cat("Applying KNN imputation directly (k=5)...\n")
    analysis_imputed <- VIM::kNN(analysis_data_raw, k = 5, numFun = median, imp_var = FALSE)
    analysis_data <- analysis_imputed
  }
} else {
  analysis_data <- analysis_data_raw
  cat("\nNo missing values found, skipping imputation.\n")
}

# Remove columns with no variance
analysis_data <- analysis_data %>%
  select(where(~ var(., na.rm = TRUE) > 0.001))

# Check final missing values
missing_after <- sum(is.na(analysis_data))
cat("\nMissing values after imputation:", missing_after, "\n")
cat("Percentage missing after:", round(missing_after / (nrow(analysis_data) * ncol(analysis_data)) * 100, 2), "%\n")

# Remove any remaining NAs
if(missing_after > 0) {
  cat("Removing remaining NA values...\n")
  analysis_data <- na.omit(analysis_data)
  cat("Rows after removing remaining NAs:", nrow(analysis_data), "\n")
}

# Check final dimensions
cat("\nFinal dataset dimensions:", dim(analysis_data), "\n")

# 1. BASIC DIGITAL PAYMENT ADOPTION RATE IN SRI LANKA
# -----------------------------------------------------
# Calculate overall digital payment adoption rate
overall_adoption <- analysis_data %>%
  summarise(
    total_respondents = n(),
    digital_payment_users = sum(anydigpayment == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    adoption_rate = mean(anydigpayment == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    non_users = sum(anydigpayment == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

cat("\n=== DIGITAL PAYMENT ADOPTION IN SRI LANKA (After KNN Imputation) ===\n")
print(overall_adoption)

# 2. DEMOGRAPHIC ANALYSIS OF DIGITAL PAYMENT ADOPTION
# -----------------------------------------------------
# Create demographic groups for analysis

# Function to calculate adoption rates by demographic
calculate_adoption_by_demo <- function(data, group_var) {
  data %>%
    group_by({{group_var}}) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      digital_users = sum(anydigpayment == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      adoption_rate = mean(anydigpayment == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
}

# 2.1 By Gender
gender_adoption <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(gender_label = case_when(
    female == 1 ~ "Female",
    female == 2 ~ "Male",
    TRUE ~ "Unknown"
  )) %>%
  calculate_adoption_by_demo(gender_label) %>%
  arrange(desc(adoption_rate))

# 2.2 By Age Group
age_adoption <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(age_group = case_when(
    age < 25 ~ "15-24",
    age >= 25 & age < 35 ~ "25-34",
    age >= 35 & age < 45 ~ "35-44",
    age >= 45 & age < 55 ~ "45-54",
    age >= 55 & age < 65 ~ "55-64",
    age >= 65 ~ "65+",
    TRUE ~ "Unknown"
  )) %>%
  calculate_adoption_by_demo(age_group) %>%
  arrange(desc(adoption_rate))

# 2.3 By Education Level
education_adoption <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(education_level = case_when(
    educ == 1 ~ "Primary or less",
    educ == 2 ~ "Secondary",
    educ == 3 ~ "Tertiary",
    TRUE ~ "Unknown"
  )) %>%
  calculate_adoption_by_demo(education_level) %>%
  arrange(desc(adoption_rate))

# 2.4 By Income Quintile
income_adoption <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(income_group = case_when(
    inc_q == 1 ~ "Poorest 20%",
    inc_q == 2 ~ "Second 20%",
    inc_q == 3 ~ "Middle 20%",
    inc_q == 4 ~ "Fourth 20%",
    inc_q == 5 ~ "Richest 20%",
    TRUE ~ "Unknown"
  )) %>%
  calculate_adoption_by_demo(income_group) %>%
  arrange(desc(adoption_rate))

# 2.5 By Employment Status
employment_adoption <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(employment_status = case_when(
    emp_in == 1 ~ "Employed",
    emp_in == 2 ~ "Not Employed",
    TRUE ~ "Unknown"
  )) %>%
  calculate_adoption_by_demo(employment_status) %>%
  arrange(desc(adoption_rate))

# 2.6 By Urban/Rural
urban_adoption <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(urbanicity_label = case_when(
    urbanicity == 1 ~ "Urban",
    urbanicity == 2 ~ "Rural",
    TRUE ~ "Unknown"
  )) %>%
  calculate_adoption_by_demo(urbanicity_label) %>%
  arrange(desc(adoption_rate))

# Print demographic results
cat("\n=== DIGITAL PAYMENT ADOPTION BY DEMOGRAPHIC GROUPS ===\n")

cat("\n1. By Gender:\n")
print(gender_adoption)

cat("\n2. By Age Group:\n")
print(age_adoption)

cat("\n3. By Education Level:\n")
print(education_adoption)

cat("\n4. By Income Quintile:\n")
print(income_adoption)

cat("\n5. By Employment Status:\n")
print(employment_adoption)

cat("\n6. By Urban/Rural:\n")
print(urban_adoption)

# 3. VISUALIZATIONS
# -----------------------------------------------------
# Set theme for better visualizations
theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 12))

# 3.1 Overall Adoption Pie Chart
overall_pie_data <- data.frame(
  Group = c("Digital Payment Users", "Non-Users"),
  Count = c(overall_adoption$digital_payment_users, overall_adoption$non_users)
) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = Count/sum(Count) * 100)

overall_pie <- ggplot(overall_pie_data, aes(x = "", y = Count, fill = Group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Percentage, 1), "%")), 
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5)) +
  labs(title = "Overall Digital Payment Adoption in Sri Lanka\n(After KNN Imputation)",
       fill = "Group") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))

# 3.2 Updated function for bar charts (FIXED VERSION)
create_demo_bar <- function(data, title, x_col) {
  # Reorder the data by adoption_rate in descending order
  data <- data %>%
    mutate({{x_col}} := fct_reorder({{x_col}}, -adoption_rate))
  
  ggplot(data, aes(x = {{x_col}}, y = adoption_rate, fill = {{x_col}})) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(adoption_rate, 1), "%")), 
              vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
    labs(title = title,
         x = "",
         y = "Adoption Rate (%)") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5)) +
    ylim(0, max(data$adoption_rate) * 1.1)
}

# Create individual bar charts using the corrected function
p1 <- create_demo_bar(gender_adoption, "Adoption by Gender", gender_label)
p2 <- create_demo_bar(age_adoption, "Adoption by Age Group", age_group)
p3 <- create_demo_bar(education_adoption, "Adoption by Education", education_level)
p4 <- create_demo_bar(income_adoption, "Adoption by Income", income_group)
p5 <- create_demo_bar(employment_adoption, "Adoption by Employment", employment_status)
p6 <- create_demo_bar(urban_adoption, "Adoption by Urbanicity", urbanicity_label)

# Display all charts
print(overall_pie)

# Arrange demographic charts
grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, ncol = 3)

# 3.3 Heatmap of Adoption by Multiple Demographics
heatmap_data <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(
    age_group = case_when(
      age < 25 ~ "15-24",
      age >= 25 & age < 35 ~ "25-34",
      age >= 35 & age < 45 ~ "35-44",
      age >= 45 & age < 55 ~ "45-54",
      age >= 55 & age < 65 ~ "55-64",
      age >= 65 ~ "65+",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    ),
    income_group = case_when(
      inc_q == 1 ~ "Poorest",
      inc_q == 2 ~ "Lower Middle",
      inc_q == 3 ~ "Middle",
      inc_q == 4 ~ "Upper Middle",
      inc_q == 5 ~ "Richest",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(age_group, income_group) %>%
  summarise(
    adoption_rate = mean(anydigpayment == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    n = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(age_group), !is.na(income_group), age_group != "Unknown", income_group != "Unknown")

# Create factor levels for proper ordering
if(nrow(heatmap_data) > 0) {
  heatmap_data <- heatmap_data %>%
    mutate(
      age_group = factor(age_group, levels = c("15-24", "25-34", "35-44", "45-54", "55-64", "65+")),
      income_group = factor(income_group, levels = c("Poorest", "Lower Middle", "Middle", "Upper Middle", "Richest"))
    )
  
  # Heatmap
  heatmap_plot <- ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = income_group, y = age_group, 
                                            fill = adoption_rate)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(adoption_rate, 1), "%")), 
              color = "black", size = 3.5) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue", 
                        name = "Adoption Rate (%)") +
    labs(title = "Digital Payment Adoption: Age vs Income\n(After KNN Imputation)",
         x = "Income Group",
         y = "Age Group") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))
  
  print(heatmap_plot)
} else {
  cat("\nNote: Insufficient data for heatmap visualization\n")
}

# 4. DIGITAL PAYMENT TYPE ANALYSIS
# -----------------------------------------------------
# Analyze specific types of digital payments
payment_types <- analysis_data %>%
  summarise(
    merchant_digital = mean(merchantpay_dig == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    instore_digital = mean(fin25e2 == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    bill_digital = mean(fin26a == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    online_purchase = mean(fin26b == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    remittance_sent = mean(fin28 == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    remittance_received = mean(fin29 == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    wage_digital = mean(fin34a == 1 | fin34b == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    govt_digital = mean(fin39a == 1 | fin39b == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "payment_type", values_to = "percentage")

# Create labels for payment types
payment_types <- payment_types %>%
  mutate(
    payment_label = case_when(
      payment_type == "merchant_digital" ~ "Merchant Payments",
      payment_type == "instore_digital" ~ "In-store Payments",
      payment_type == "bill_digital" ~ "Bill Payments",
      payment_type == "online_purchase" ~ "Online Purchases",
      payment_type == "remittance_sent" ~ "Remittances Sent",
      payment_type == "remittance_received" ~ "Remittances Received",
      payment_type == "wage_digital" ~ "Digital Wage Payments",
      payment_type == "govt_digital" ~ "Digital Govt Payments",
      TRUE ~ payment_type
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(percentage))

# Plot payment type adoption
payment_plot <- ggplot(payment_types, 
                       aes(x = reorder(payment_label, -percentage), 
                           y = percentage, fill = payment_label)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(percentage, 1), "%")), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
  labs(title = "Adoption of Different Digital Payment Types in Sri Lanka\n(After KNN Imputation)",
       x = "Payment Type",
       y = "Percentage of Population (%)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14)) +
  ylim(0, max(payment_types$percentage) * 1.1)

print(payment_plot)

# 5. DIGITAL ENABLERS ANALYSIS
# -----------------------------------------------------
# Analyze factors that enable digital payments
enablers <- analysis_data %>%
  summarise(
    has_account = mean(account == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    has_digital_account = mean(dig_account == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    used_card_phone = mean(fin3 == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    owns_mobile = mean(con1 == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    internet_use = mean(internet_use == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    has_smartphone = mean(con9 == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "enabler", values_to = "percentage") %>%
  mutate(
    enabler_label = case_when(
      enabler == "has_account" ~ "Has Any Account",
      enabler == "has_digital_account" ~ "Has Digital Account",
      enabler == "used_card_phone" ~ "Used Card/Phone",
      enabler == "owns_mobile" ~ "Owns Mobile Phone",
      enabler == "internet_use" ~ "Uses Internet",
      enabler == "has_smartphone" ~ "Has Smartphone",
      TRUE ~ enabler
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(percentage))

# Plot enablers
enablers_plot <- ggplot(enablers, 
                        aes(x = reorder(enabler_label, -percentage), 
                            y = percentage, fill = enabler_label)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(percentage, 1), "%")), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
  labs(title = "Digital Payment Enablers in Sri Lanka\n(After KNN Imputation)",
       x = "Enabler",
       y = "Percentage of Population (%)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14)) +
  ylim(0, max(enablers$percentage) * 1.1)

print(enablers_plot)

# 6. PREDICTIVE ANALYSIS - DECISION TREE
# -----------------------------------------------------
# Prepare data for decision tree
tree_data <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(
    digital_payment = factor(anydigpayment, levels = c(0, 1), 
                            labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    gender = factor(female, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Female", "Male")),
    age_group = cut(age, 
                    breaks = c(15, 25, 35, 45, 55, 65, 100),
                    labels = c("15-24", "25-34", "35-44", "45-54", "55-64", "65+"),
                    include.lowest = TRUE),
    education = factor(educ, levels = c(1, 2, 3), 
                      labels = c("Primary", "Secondary", "Tertiary")),
    income = factor(inc_q, levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),
                   labels = c("Poorest", "Poor", "Middle", "Rich", "Richest")),
    employed = factor(emp_in, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Yes", "No")),
    urban = factor(urbanicity, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Urban", "Rural")),
    has_account = factor(account, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    has_mobile = factor(con1, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    uses_internet = factor(internet_use, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes"))
  ) %>%
  select(digital_payment, gender, age_group, education, income, 
         employed, urban, has_account, has_mobile, uses_internet) %>%
  na.omit()

# Build decision tree only if we have enough data
if(nrow(tree_data) > 50) {
  set.seed(123)
  tree_model <- rpart(digital_payment ~ ., 
                      data = tree_data, 
                      method = "class",
                      control = rpart.control(cp = 0.01, minsplit = 20))
  
  # Plot decision tree
  rpart.plot(tree_model, 
             type = 4, 
             extra = 104,
             box.palette = "BuGn",
             fallen.leaves = TRUE,
             main = "Decision Tree: Predictors of Digital Payment Adoption\n(After KNN Imputation)")
  
  # Print variable importance
  var_importance <- tree_model$variable.importance
  cat("\n=== VARIABLE IMPORTANCE FOR DIGITAL PAYMENT ADOPTION (After KNN Imputation) ===\n")
  print(sort(var_importance, decreasing = TRUE))
} else {
  cat("\nInsufficient data for decision tree analysis\n")
}

# 7. CORRELATION ANALYSIS
# -----------------------------------------------------
# Calculate correlations between key variables
correlation_data <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(
    digital_payment = anydigpayment,
    age = age,
    education = educ,
    income = inc_q,
    has_account = account,
    has_mobile = con1,
    uses_internet = internet_use,
    borrowed = borrowed,
    saved = saved
  ) %>%
  select(digital_payment, age, education, income, has_account, 
         has_mobile, uses_internet, borrowed, saved) %>%
  na.omit()

# Only create correlation matrix if we have enough data
if(nrow(correlation_data) > 10) {
  cor_matrix <- cor(correlation_data)
  
  # Create correlation heatmap
  cor_heatmap_data <- as.data.frame(cor_matrix) %>%
    rownames_to_column(var = "var1") %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -var1, names_to = "var2", values_to = "correlation")
  
  cor_heatmap <- ggplot(cor_heatmap_data, aes(x = var1, y = var2, fill = correlation)) +
    geom_tile() +
    geom_text(aes(label = round(correlation, 2)), color = "white", size = 3) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", mid = "white", high = "blue",
                        midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1)) +
    labs(title = "Correlation Matrix of Key Variables\n(After KNN Imputation)",
         x = "",
         y = "") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))
  
  print(cor_heatmap)
} else {
  cat("\nInsufficient data for correlation analysis\n")
}

# 8. SUMMARY STATISTICS
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("COMPREHENSIVE SUMMARY: DIGITAL PAYMENT ADOPTION ANALYSIS (AFTER KNN IMPUTATION)\n")
cat(paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

cat("1. OVERALL ADOPTION STATISTICS:\n")
cat("   Total Respondents: ", overall_adoption$total_respondents, "\n")
cat("   Digital Payment Users: ", overall_adoption$digital_payment_users, "\n")
cat("   Adoption Rate: ", round(overall_adoption$adoption_rate, 1), "%\n")
cat("   Non-Users: ", overall_adoption$non_users, "\n\n")

cat("2. DEMOGRAPHIC BREAKDOWN:\n")

cat("   Highest Adoption by Category:\n")
cat("   • Gender: ", gender_adoption$gender_label[1], " (", 
    round(gender_adoption$adoption_rate[1], 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
cat("   • Age Group: ", age_adoption$age_group[1], " (", 
    round(age_adoption$adoption_rate[1], 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
cat("   • Education: ", education_adoption$education_level[1], " (", 
    round(education_adoption$adoption_rate[1], 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
cat("   • Income: ", income_adoption$income_group[1], " (", 
    round(income_adoption$adoption_rate[1], 1), "%)\n", sep = "")

cat("\n   Lowest Adoption by Category:\n")
cat("   • Gender: ", tail(gender_adoption$gender_label, 1), " (", 
    round(tail(gender_adoption$adoption_rate, 1), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
cat("   • Age Group: ", tail(age_adoption$age_group, 1), " (", 
    round(tail(age_adoption$adoption_rate, 1), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
cat("   • Education: ", tail(education_adoption$education_level, 1), " (", 
    round(tail(education_adoption$adoption_rate, 1), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
cat("   • Income: ", tail(income_adoption$income_group, 1), " (", 
    round(tail(income_adoption$adoption_rate, 1), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")

cat("\n3. URBAN-RURAL DIVIDE:\n")
urban_rate <- urban_adoption$adoption_rate[urban_adoption$urbanicity_label == "Urban"]
rural_rate <- urban_adoption$adoption_rate[urban_adoption$urbanicity_label == "Rural"]

if(length(urban_rate) > 0 && length(rural_rate) > 0) {
  cat("   Urban Adoption: ", round(urban_rate, 1), "%\n", sep = "")
  cat("   Rural Adoption: ", round(rural_rate, 1), "%\n", sep = "")
  cat("   Urban-Rural Gap: ", round(urban_rate - rural_rate, 1), 
      " percentage points\n", sep = "")
} else {
  cat("   Urban/Rural data not available\n")
}

cat("\n4. KEY DIGITAL ENABLERS:\n")
if(nrow(enablers) > 0) {
  for(i in 1:min(3, nrow(enablers))) {
    cat("   ", i, ". ", enablers$enabler_label[i], ": ", 
        round(enablers$percentage[i], 1), "%\n", sep = "")
  }
} else {
  cat("   No enabler data available\n")
}

cat("\n5. MOST POPULAR PAYMENT METHODS:\n")
if(nrow(payment_types) > 0) {
  for(i in 1:min(3, nrow(payment_types))) {
    cat("   ", i, ". ", payment_types$payment_label[i], ": ", 
        round(payment_types$percentage[i], 1), "%\n", sep = "")
  }
} else {
  cat("   No payment type data available\n")
}

cat("\n6. KEY INSIGHTS:\n")
# Calculate gaps if data exists
if(nrow(gender_adoption) > 1 && nrow(age_adoption) > 1 && nrow(income_adoption) > 1) {
  gender_gap <- abs(gender_adoption$adoption_rate[1] - tail(gender_adoption$adoption_rate, 1))
  age_gap <- abs(age_adoption$adoption_rate[1] - tail(age_adoption$adoption_rate, 1))
  income_gap <- abs(income_adoption$adoption_rate[1] - tail(income_adoption$adoption_rate, 1))
  
  cat("   • Largest demographic gap: ")
  if(income_gap >= gender_gap & income_gap >= age_gap) {
    cat("Income gap (", round(income_gap, 1), " percentage points)\n", sep = "")
  } else if(gender_gap >= age_gap) {
    cat("Gender gap (", round(gender_gap, 1), " percentage points)\n", sep = "")
  } else {
    cat("Age gap (", round(age_gap, 1), " percentage points)\n", sep = "")
  }
} else {
  cat("   • Insufficient data to calculate demographic gaps\n")
}

# Identify top barrier
if(nrow(enablers) > 0) {
  lowest_enabler <- enablers %>% slice_min(percentage, n = 1)
  cat("   • Key barrier to adoption: ", lowest_enabler$enabler_label[1], 
      " (only ", round(lowest_enabler$percentage[1], 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
} else {
  cat("   • Cannot identify key barrier (no enabler data)\n")
}

cat("\n7. POLICY RECOMMENDATIONS:\n")
if(nrow(age_adoption) > 0) {
  cat("   1. Target ", tail(age_adoption$age_group, 1), " age group with digital literacy programs\n", sep = "")
}
if(nrow(income_adoption) > 0) {
  cat("   2. Focus on ", tail(income_adoption$income_group, 1), " income group for financial inclusion\n", sep = "")
}
cat("   3. Address the urban-rural gap through infrastructure development\n")
if(nrow(enablers) > 0) {
  cat("   4. Promote ", enablers$enabler_label[which.min(enablers$percentage)], " to enable digital payments\n", sep = "")
}
if(nrow(payment_types) > 0) {
  cat("   5. Encourage use of ", payment_types$payment_label[which.max(payment_types$percentage)], "\n", sep = "")
}
cat("   6. Implement targeted awareness campaigns for non-users\n")
cat("   7. Develop simplified digital payment solutions for older populations\n")

cat("\n", paste0(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n", sep = "")

# 9. EXPORT RESULTS
# -----------------------------------------------------
# Export to CSV
if(nrow(gender_adoption) > 0) {
  write.csv(gender_adoption, "gender_adoption_sri_lanka_knn.csv", row.names = FALSE)
}
if(nrow(age_adoption) > 0) {
  write.csv(age_adoption, "age_adoption_sri_lanka_knn.csv", row.names = FALSE)
}
if(nrow(income_adoption) > 0) {
  write.csv(income_adoption, "income_adoption_sri_lanka_knn.csv", row.names = FALSE)
}
if(nrow(payment_types) > 0) {
  write.csv(payment_types, "payment_types_sri_lanka_knn.csv", row.names = FALSE)
}

cat("\nResults exported to CSV files (After KNN Imputation):\n")
cat("  • gender_adoption_sri_lanka_knn.csv\n")
cat("  • age_adoption_sri_lanka_knn.csv\n")
cat("  • income_adoption_sri_lanka_knn.csv\n")
cat("  • payment_types_sri_lanka_knn.csv\n")

# 13. CREATE A FINAL REPORT
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n")
cat("This analysis provides comprehensive insights into digital payment adoption in Sri Lanka.\n")
cat("Data was preprocessed using KNN imputation (k=5) to handle missing values.\n")
cat("Key findings have been exported to CSV files for further analysis.\n")
cat("Visualizations have been generated to illustrate the adoption patterns.\n\n")

cat("DATA PROCESSING SUMMARY:\n")
cat("• Initial missing values: ", missing_before, " (", 
    round(missing_before/total_cells*100, 2), "%)\n", sep = "")
cat("• Final missing values after KNN imputation: ", missing_after, " (", 
    round(missing_after/(nrow(analysis_data)*ncol(analysis_data))*100, 2), "%)\n", sep = "")
cat("• Final sample size: ", nrow(analysis_data), " respondents\n", sep = "")
cat("• Number of variables analyzed: ", ncol(analysis_data), "\n", sep = "")

cat("\nNEXT STEPS:\n")
cat("1. Review the generated CSV files for detailed statistics\n")
cat("2. Examine the visualizations to identify patterns and gaps\n")
cat("3. Use the insights for policy development and program planning\n")
cat("4. Consider conducting qualitative research to understand barriers\n")
cat("5. Monitor adoption trends over time with future surveys\n")
```
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(VIM)

# Read the data
data <- read.csv("findex_microdata_2025_labelled.csv")

# Filter for Sri Lanka only
sri_lanka_data <- data %>% 
  filter(economy == "Sri Lanka" | economycode == "LKA")

# Select key variables for analysis
key_vars <- c(
  # Demographic variables
  "anydigpayment", "female", "age", "educ", "inc_q", "emp_in", "urbanicity",
  
  # Digital payment types
  "merchantpay_dig", "fin25e2", "fin25e3", "fin26a", "fin26b", 
  "fin28", "fin29", "fin34a", "fin34b", "fin39a", "fin39b",
  
  # Digital enablers
  "account", "dig_account", "fin3", "con1", "con9", "internet_use",
  
  # Financial behavior
  "borrowed", "saved"
)

# Check which variables exist in the dataset
available_vars <- key_vars[key_vars %in% colnames(sri_lanka_data)]
cat("Available variables for analysis:", length(available_vars), "out of", length(key_vars), "\n")

# Select and prepare data
analysis_data_raw <- sri_lanka_data %>%
  select(all_of(available_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric))

# Apply KNN imputation for missing values
if(sum(is.na(analysis_data_raw)) > 0) {
  cat("Applying KNN imputation for missing values (k=5)...\n")
  # Use sampling strategy for large datasets
  if(nrow(analysis_data_raw) > 10000) {
    set.seed(123)
    sample_size <- min(10000, nrow(analysis_data_raw))
    sample_indices <- sample(1:nrow(analysis_data_raw), sample_size)
    data_sample <- analysis_data_raw[sample_indices, ]
    
    # Apply KNN on sample
    data_sample_imputed <- VIM::kNN(data_sample, k = 5, numFun = median, imp_var = FALSE)
    
    # Calculate imputation values from sample
    column_impute_values <- list()
    for(col in colnames(data_sample_imputed)) {
      unique_vals <- unique(na.omit(data_sample_imputed[[col]]))
      if(length(unique_vals) <= 2) {
        mode_val <- as.numeric(names(sort(table(data_sample_imputed[[col]]), decreasing = TRUE))[1])
        column_impute_values[[col]] <- mode_val
      } else {
        column_impute_values[[col]] <- median(data_sample_imputed[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)
      }
    }
    
    # Apply imputation to full dataset
    analysis_data <- analysis_data_raw
    for(col in colnames(analysis_data)) {
      if(col %in% names(column_impute_values)) {
        analysis_data[[col]][is.na(analysis_data[[col]])] <- column_impute_values[[col]]
      }
    }
  } else {
    analysis_data <- VIM::kNN(analysis_data_raw, k = 5, numFun = median, imp_var = FALSE)
  }
} else {
  analysis_data <- analysis_data_raw
}

# Remove any remaining NAs
analysis_data <- na.omit(analysis_data)

# Create meaningful labels for categorical variables
analysis_data <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(
    # Digital payment adoption
    anydigpayment_label = factor(anydigpayment, 
                                 levels = c(0, 1), 
                                 labels = c("No Digital Payment", "Uses Digital Payment")),
    
    # Gender
    female_label = factor(female,
                          levels = c(1, 2),
                          labels = c("Female", "Male")),
    
    # Education
    educ_label = factor(educ,
                        levels = c(1, 2, 3),
                        labels = c("Primary or less", "Secondary", "Tertiary")),
    
    # Income quintile
    inc_q_label = factor(inc_q,
                         levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),
                         labels = c("Poorest 20%", "Second 20%", "Middle 20%", "Fourth 20%", "Richest 20%")),
    
    # Employment
    emp_in_label = factor(emp_in,
                          levels = c(1, 2),
                          labels = c("Employed", "Not Employed")),
    
    # Urbanicity
    urbanicity_label = factor(urbanicity,
                              levels = c(1, 2),
                              labels = c("Urban", "Rural")),
    
    # Financial behavior
    borrowed_label = factor(borrowed,
                            levels = c(0, 1),
                            labels = c("Not Borrowed", "Borrowed")),
    
    saved_label = factor(saved,
                         levels = c(0, 1),
                         labels = c("Not Saved", "Saved")),
    
    # Digital account
    account_label = factor(account,
                           levels = c(0, 1),
                           labels = c("No Account", "Has Account")),
    
    # Digital account type
    dig_account_label = factor(dig_account,
                               levels = c(0, 1),
                               labels = c("Non-Digital Account", "Digital Account")),
    
    # Mobile ownership
    con1_label = factor(con1,
                        levels = c(0, 1),
                        labels = c("No Mobile", "Has Mobile")),
    
    # Smartphone ownership
    con9_label = factor(con9,
                        levels = c(0, 1),
                        labels = c("No Smartphone", "Has Smartphone")),
    
    # Internet use
    internet_use_label = factor(internet_use,
                                levels = c(0, 1),
                                labels = c("No Internet", "Uses Internet"))
  )

cat("\n=== BASE R BOXPLOTS FOR CATEGORICAL VARIABLES ===\n")

# 1. BOX PLOTS FOR CONTINUOUS VARIABLES BY CATEGORICAL GROUPS
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))  # Set up 2x2 plot layout

# Box plot 1: Age by Gender
boxplot(age ~ female_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Gender",
        xlab = "Gender", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightpink", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 2: Age by Education Level
boxplot(age ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Education Level",
        xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 3: Age by Income Quintile
boxplot(age ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Income Quintile",
        xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Age",
        col = rainbow(5),
        border = "darkred",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 4: Age by Digital Payment Adoption
boxplot(age ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Digital Payment Adoption",
        xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# 2. MORE BOX PLOTS
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))  # Reset layout

# Box plot 5: Age by Employment Status
boxplot(age ~ emp_in_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Employment Status",
        xlab = "Employment Status", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgreen", "lightcoral"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 6: Age by Urban/Rural
boxplot(age ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Urban/Rural",
        xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 7: Age by Borrowing Status
boxplot(age ~ borrowed_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Borrowing Status",
        xlab = "Borrowed in Past Year", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 8: Age by Saving Status
boxplot(age ~ saved_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Saving Status",
        xlab = "Saved in Past Year", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# 3. BOX PLOTS FOR DIGITAL PAYMENT VARIABLES
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(3, 2))  # Set up 3x2 layout

# Create box plots for digital payment adoption by various factors
if("merchantpay_dig" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(merchantpay_dig ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Merchant Payments by Overall Adoption",
          xlab = "Overall Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Digital Merchant Payments",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin25e2" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin25e2 ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "In-store Digital Payments by Education",
          xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "In-store Digital Payments",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin26a" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin26a ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Bill Payments by Income",
          xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Digital Bill Payments",
          col = rainbow(5),
          border = "darkred")
}

if("fin26b" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin26b ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Online Purchases by Urban/Rural",
          xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Online Purchases",
          col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("fin28" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin28 ~ emp_in_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Remittances Sent by Employment",
          xlab = "Employment Status", ylab = "Digital Remittances Sent",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin29" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin29 ~ female_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Remittances Received by Gender",
          xlab = "Gender", ylab = "Digital Remittances Received",
          col = c("lightpink", "lightblue"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

# 4. BOX PLOTS FOR DIGITAL ENABLERS
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(3, 2))  # Set up 3x2 layout

if("account" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(account ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Bank Account Ownership by Digital Payment Adoption",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Has Bank Account",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("dig_account" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(dig_account ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Account Ownership by Digital Payment Adoption",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Has Digital Account",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("con1" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(con1 ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Mobile Phone Ownership by Income",
          xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Owns Mobile Phone",
          col = rainbow(5),
          border = "darkred")
}

if("con9" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(con9 ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Smartphone Ownership by Education",
          xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "Has Smartphone",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("internet_use" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(internet_use ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Internet Use by Urban/Rural",
          xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Uses Internet",
          col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("fin3" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin3 ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Card/Phone Payment Use by Digital Payment Adoption",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Used Card/Phone for Payment",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkred")
}

# 5. COMPREHENSIVE BOX PLOT ANALYSIS
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== COMPREHENSIVE BOX PLOT ANALYSIS ===\n")

# Function to create detailed box plot with statistics
create_detailed_boxplot <- function(data, y_var, x_var, y_label, x_label, title) {
  par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
  
  # Create box plot
  boxplot(as.formula(paste(y_var, "~", x_var)), data = data,
          main = title,
          xlab = x_label, ylab = y_label,
          col = "lightblue",
          border = "darkblue",
          notch = TRUE,
          outline = TRUE)
  
  # Add mean points
  means <- tapply(data[[y_var]], data[[x_var]], mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  points(1:length(means), means, col = "red", pch = 19, cex = 1.2)
  legend("topright", legend = "Mean", col = "red", pch = 19)
  
  # Create summary statistics table
  stats_summary <- data %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(x_var))) %>%
    summarise(
      Mean = round(mean(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Median = round(median(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      SD = round(sd(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Min = round(min(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Max = round(max(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Count = n(),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  # Create empty plot for table
  plot(1, type = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "", xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1),
       axes = FALSE, main = "Summary Statistics")
  
  # Add table to plot - FIXED VERSION
  # Start position for text
  y_pos <- 0.9
  
  # Title
  text(0.1, y_pos, "Summary Statistics:", cex = 1.2, font = 2)
  y_pos <- y_pos - 0.05
  
  # Column headers
  text(0.1, y_pos, paste(names(stats_summary), collapse = "  "), cex = 0.8, adj = 0, font = 2)
  y_pos <- y_pos - 0.05
  
  # Add each row of data
  for(i in 1:nrow(stats_summary)) {
    row_text <- paste(format(stats_summary[i, ], nsmall = 2), collapse = "  ")
    text(0.1, y_pos, row_text, cex = 0.8, adj = 0)
    y_pos <- y_pos - 0.05
    
    # Break if running out of space
    if(y_pos < 0.1) break
  }
}

# OR EVEN SIMPLER - Just print the table to console instead of plotting
create_detailed_boxplot_simple <- function(data, y_var, x_var, y_label, x_label, title) {
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
  
  # Create box plot
  boxplot(as.formula(paste(y_var, "~", x_var)), data = data,
          main = title,
          xlab = x_label, ylab = y_label,
          col = "lightblue",
          border = "darkblue",
          notch = TRUE,
          outline = TRUE)
  
  # Add mean points
  means <- tapply(data[[y_var]], data[[x_var]], mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  points(1:length(means), means, col = "red", pch = 19, cex = 1.2)
  legend("topright", legend = "Mean", col = "red", pch = 19)
  
  # Create and print summary statistics table to console
  cat("\nSummary Statistics for:", title, "\n")
  stats_summary <- data %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(x_var))) %>%
    summarise(
      Mean = round(mean(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Median = round(median(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      SD = round(sd(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Min = round(min(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Max = round(max(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Count = n(),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  print(stats_summary)
  cat("\n")
}

# Create detailed box plots for key variables using the simpler version
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "educ_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  cat("\n1. Detailed Age Analysis by Education Level:\n")
  create_detailed_boxplot_simple(analysis_data, "age", "educ_label", 
                                "Age", "Education Level", 
                                "Age Distribution by Education Level")
}

if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  cat("\n2. Detailed Age Analysis by Digital Payment Adoption:\n")
  create_detailed_boxplot_simple(analysis_data, "age", "anydigpayment_label",
                                "Age", "Digital Payment Adoption",
                                "Age Distribution by Digital Payment Adoption")
}

# Or if you want to keep it in the PDF, use this alternative approach:
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "educ_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
  
  # Box plot
  boxplot(age ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Age Distribution by Education Level",
          xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "Age",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen",
          notch = TRUE)
  
  # Add mean points
  means <- tapply(analysis_data$age, analysis_data$educ_label, mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  points(1:length(means), means, col = "red", pch = 19, cex = 1.2)
  legend("topright", legend = "Mean", col = "red", pch = 19)
  
  # Summary table (just calculate and display in next plot)
  stats_summary <- analysis_data %>%
    group_by(educ_label) %>%
    summarise(
      Mean = round(mean(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      Median = round(median(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      SD = round(sd(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      Count = n(),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  # Create text plot
  plot(1, type = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "", xlim = c(0, 10), ylim = c(0, 10),
       axes = FALSE, main = "Summary Statistics")
  
  # Add text
  text(1, 9, "Education Level", adj = 0, font = 2)
  text(4, 9, "Mean", adj = 0, font = 2)
  text(6, 9, "Median", adj = 0, font = 2)
  text(8, 9, "Count", adj = 0, font = 2)
  
  y_pos <- 8
  for(i in 1:nrow(stats_summary)) {
    text(1, y_pos, as.character(stats_summary$educ_label[i]), adj = 0)
    text(4, y_pos, as.character(stats_summary$Mean[i]), adj = 0)
    text(6, y_pos, as.character(stats_summary$Median[i]), adj = 0)
    text(8, y_pos, as.character(stats_summary$Count[i]), adj = 0)
    y_pos <- y_pos - 1
  }
}

# 6. MULTIPLE BOX PLOTS IN SINGLE FIGURE
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))  # Reset to single plot

# Create a matrix of box plots for age by multiple categorical variables
cat("\n3. Multiple Box Plots Comparison:\n")

# Prepare data for multiple box plots
age_data_list <- list(
  by_gender = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$female_label == "Female"],
  by_education_primary = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$educ_label == "Primary or less"],
  by_education_secondary = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$educ_label == "Secondary"],
  by_education_tertiary = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$educ_label == "Tertiary"],
  by_urban = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$urbanicity_label == "Urban"],
  by_rural = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$urbanicity_label == "Rural"]
)

# Create multiple box plots
boxplot(age_data_list,
        main = "Age Distribution Across Multiple Categories",
        ylab = "Age",
        names = c("Female", "Primary", "Secondary", "Tertiary", "Urban", "Rural"),
        col = c("lightpink", "lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral", "lightblue", "lightgray"),
        border = "darkblue",
        las = 2)  # Rotate x-axis labels

# Add horizontal line for overall mean
abline(h = mean(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
legend("topright", legend = paste("Overall Mean:", round(mean(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
       col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)

# 7. SIDE-BY-SIDE COMPARISON BOX PLOTS
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))  # Two plots side by side

# Compare two important variables
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "anydigpayment" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  
  # Digital payment users vs non-users
  digital_users_age <- analysis_data$age[analysis_data$anydigpayment == 1]
  non_users_age <- analysis_data$age[analysis_data$anydigpayment == 0]
  
  boxplot(list("Non-Users" = non_users_age, "Digital Users" = digital_users_age),
          main = "Age: Digital Payment Users vs Non-Users",
          ylab = "Age",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
          border = "darkgreen")
  
  # Add statistical test annotation
  t_test <- t.test(digital_users_age, non_users_age)
  mtext(paste("p-value:", format.pval(t_test$p.value, digits = 3)), 
        side = 3, line = 0.5, cex = 0.8)
}

# Compare by gender
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "female" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  female_age <- analysis_data$age[analysis_data$female == 1]
  male_age <- analysis_data$age[analysis_data$female == 2]
  
  boxplot(list("Female" = female_age, "Male" = male_age),
          main = "Age: Female vs Male",
          ylab = "Age",
          col = c("lightpink", "lightblue"),
          border = "darkblue")
  
  # Add statistical test annotation
  t_test_gender <- t.test(female_age, male_age)
  mtext(paste("p-value:", format.pval(t_test_gender$p.value, digits = 3)), 
        side = 3, line = 0.5, cex = 0.8)
}

# 8. EXPORT ALL BOX PLOTS TO PDF
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== EXPORTING BOX PLOTS TO PDF ===\n")

# Save all box plots to PDF
pdf("sri_lanka_baseR_boxplots.pdf", width = 11, height = 8.5)

# Page 1: Age distribution plots
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
boxplot(age ~ female_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Gender",
        xlab = "Gender", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightpink", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Education Level",
        xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Income Quintile",
        xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Age",
        col = rainbow(5),
        border = "darkred",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Digital Payment Adoption",
        xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# Page 2: More demographic comparisons
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
boxplot(age ~ emp_in_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Employment Status",
        xlab = "Employment Status", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgreen", "lightcoral"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Urban/Rural",
        xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ borrowed_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Borrowing Status",
        xlab = "Borrowed in Past Year", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ saved_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Saving Status",
        xlab = "Saved in Past Year", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# Page 3: Digital payment variables
par(mfrow = c(3, 2))
if("merchantpay_dig" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(merchantpay_dig ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Merchant Payments",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Digital Merchant Payments",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin25e2" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin25e2 ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "In-store Digital Payments",
          xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "In-store Digital Payments",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin26a" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin26a ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Bill Payments",
          xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Digital Bill Payments",
          col = rainbow(5),
          border = "darkred")
}

if("fin26b" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin26b ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Online Purchases",
          xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Online Purchases",
          col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("fin28" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin28 ~ emp_in_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Remittances Sent",
          xlab = "Employment Status", ylab = "Digital Remittances Sent",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin29" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin29 ~ female_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Remittances Received",
          xlab = "Gender", ylab = "Digital Remittances Received",
          col = c("lightpink", "lightblue"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

# Page 4: Digital enablers
par(mfrow = c(3, 2))
if("account" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(account ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Bank Account Ownership",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Has Bank Account",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("dig_account" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(dig_account ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Account Ownership",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Has Digital Account",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("con1" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(con1 ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Mobile Phone Ownership",
          xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Owns Mobile Phone",
          col = rainbow(5),
          border = "darkred")
}

if("con9" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(con9 ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Smartphone Ownership",
          xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "Has Smartphone",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("internet_use" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(internet_use ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Internet Use",
          xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Uses Internet",
          col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("fin3" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin3 ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Card/Phone Payment Use",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Used Card/Phone",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkred")
}

dev.off()

cat("\nAll base R box plots saved to 'sri_lanka_baseR_boxplots.pdf'\n")

# 9. SUMMARY STATISTICS FOR BOX PLOTS
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== SUMMARY STATISTICS ===\n")

# Calculate summary statistics for key variables
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  cat("\n1. Overall Age Statistics:\n")
  cat("   Mean age:", round(mean(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "\n")
  cat("   Median age:", round(median(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "\n")
  cat("   Standard deviation:", round(sd(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "\n")
  cat("   Minimum age:", min(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("   Maximum age:", max(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("   Number of observations:", length(analysis_data$age), "\n")
}

# Age statistics by digital payment adoption
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  cat("\n2. Age Statistics by Digital Payment Adoption:\n")
  age_stats <- analysis_data %>%
    group_by(anydigpayment_label) %>%
    summarise(
      Mean = round(mean(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      Median = round(median(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      SD = round(sd(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      Min = min(age, na.rm = TRUE),
      Max = max(age, na.rm = TRUE),
      N = n(),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  print(age_stats)
}

cat("\n=== ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n")
cat("Base R box plots have been created for all categorical variables.\n")
cat("PDF report saved with all box plots.\n")
cat("Check 'sri_lanka_baseR_boxplots.pdf' for visualizations.\n")
```
